heading: Establishment clause unaffected
label: 2000bb–4 Establishment clause unaffected
text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to affect, interpret, or in any way address that portion of the First Amendment prohibiting laws respecting the establishment of religion (referred to in this section as the “Establishment Clause”). Granting government funding, benefits, or exemptions, to the extent permissible under the Establishment Clause, shall not constitute a violation of this chapter. As used in this section, the term “granting”, used with respect to government funding, benefits, or exemptions, does not include the denial of government funding, benefits, or exemptions.
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , which is classified principally to this chapter.
